title: Reinforcement Learning - Training / Agent environment with leverage
author: Dipluz
selftext:
Hey folks.

I have been developing a Reinforcement Learning model for trading. Its coming a long fairly good and I wonder if other people has implemented leverage into the agent/training environment? One of the issues ive found so far is that I can train and run the agent with the strategy just fine. 

Though lets say I give it a profit\_aim set to 2% that's cool, though if I apply 10x leverage (as an example), then the agent when I go live or run a forward test starts taking profit at 2% leveraged profit (on 10x leverage) and not the raw 2% price move that would have resulted into a 20% win. 

Anyone had some luck in this area?

comments.author: Emotional-Bee-474
comments.body:
You need to apply leverage at position sizing step. So if you normally trade with 2% risk per position just apply the leverage there, so now your position size is 20% (which I think is mad) . Then when exiting you are again exiting for say 2R but with the leverage already baked into the position size when the trade was executed. 

I am not sure if understand your issue correctly though. Leverage normally just gives you more buying power for more risk of ruin.

replies.author: Dipluz
replies.body:
Thank you for answering me, This is not quite my issue, my issue is:

  
I want**:** A 2% move on the chart. With 10x leverage, this equals a 20% gain on my margin.

The Agent Sees**:** A PnL number. My system ofcourse feeds it leveraged PnL(since leverage is turned on, and I cant change this as I am using Freqtrade/FreqAI), it sees "+2%" when the price has only moved 0.2%.
